cards.title: Chromotherapy — Principles & Scientific Basis

alerts.label: Definition
alerts.text: Chromotherapy (colour therapy) uses specific wavelengths of visible light (380–700nm) to influence biological processes via photoreceptors, photobiomodulation, and neuroendocrine pathways.

rows: Stimulant, vasodilatory, increases circulation and metabolism
Nerve stimulant, digestive tonic, lymphatic activator
Diabetes, constipation, liver disorders, skin conditions
Eye disorders, ear problems, bleeding, mental disorders

pearls.label: 🟡 Pearl
pearls.text: Blue light (460nm) is the most evidence-based — used clinically for neonatal jaundice (phototherapy), SAD (seasonal affective disorder), and acne vulgaris (kills P. acnes). Red light (630–850nm) photobiomodulation has RCT evidence for wound healing, muscle recovery, and joint pain.

cards._legacyHtml:
Definition
Chromotherapy (colour therapy) uses specific wavelengths of visible light (380–700nm) to influence biological processes via photoreceptors, photobiomodulation, and neuroendocrine pathways.
ColourWavelengthPrimary EffectsKey Indications
🔴 Red620–700nmStimulant, vasodilatory, increases circulation and metabolismAnaemia, fatigue, paralysis, arthritis, depression
🟠 Orange590–620nmAntispasmodic, appetite stimulant, lung tonicBronchitis, asthma, hypothyroidism, constipation
🟡 Yellow570–590nmNerve stimulant, digestive tonic, lymphatic activatorDiabetes, constipation, liver disorders, skin conditions
🟢 Green495–570nmBalancing, antiseptic, pituitary stimulantHypertension, ulcers, headache, nervous disorders
🔵 Blue450–495nmSedative, anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, antisepticFever, inflammation, insomnia, hyperthyroidism, pain
🟣 Indigo425–450nmPain relief, haemostatic, muscle relaxantEye disorders, ear problems, bleeding, mental disorders
🔮 Violet380–425nmLymphatic stimulant, anti-tumour, spiritualNeurological conditions, scalp disorders, epilepsy
🟡 Pearl
Blue light (460nm) is the most evidence-based — used clinically for neonatal jaundice (phototherapy), SAD (seasonal affective disorder), and acne vulgaris (kills P. acnes). Red light (630–850nm) photobiomodulation has RCT evidence for wound healing, muscle recovery, and joint pain.

cards.title: Chromotherapy — Application Methods & Duration

steps.title: Colour Lamps
steps.text: — Incandescent bulb with colour filter/gel. Patient exposed at 60–90cm distance. Duration: 20–30 min per session. Most common method in naturopathy clinics.

steps.title: Solarised Water
steps.text: — Water charged in coloured glass bottle in sunlight for 4–6 hours. Prescribed as drinks (50–100ml TDS) or external application. Used for systemic effects.

steps.title: Colour Breathing
steps.text: — Visualisation of breathing in specific colours during pranayama. Combined with trataka for mind-body integration.

steps.title: Colour Silk/Cloth
steps.text: — Coloured cloth placed on affected body part. Allows gentle colour penetration. Used for local conditions.

steps.title: LED Therapy
steps.text: — Modern evidence-based photobiomodulation. Red (630nm) for tissue healing. Blue (415nm) for acne. Near-infrared (850nm) for deep tissue.

cards._legacyHtml:
1
Colour Lamps — Incandescent bulb with colour filter/gel. Patient exposed at 60–90cm distance. Duration: 20–30 min per session. Most common method in naturopathy clinics.
2
Solarised Water — Water charged in coloured glass bottle in sunlight for 4–6 hours. Prescribed as drinks (50–100ml TDS) or external application. Used for systemic effects.
3
Colour Breathing — Visualisation of breathing in specific colours during pranayama. Combined with trataka for mind-body integration.
4
Colour Silk/Cloth — Coloured cloth placed on affected body part. Allows gentle colour penetration. Used for local conditions.
5
LED Therapy — Modern evidence-based photobiomodulation. Red (630nm) for tissue healing. Blue (415nm) for acne. Near-infrared (850nm) for deep tissue.
ConditionColourMethodDurationFrequency
InsomniaBlueLamp30 minDaily at night
Depression/FatigueYellow/OrangeLamp20 minMorning daily
HypertensionBlue/GreenLamp + water25 minDaily

cards.title: Chromotherapy — Contraindications & Safety

alerts.label: Absolute Contraindications
alerts.text: Never apply red/infrared light to: active cancer sites, acute inflammation/infection, areas of active bleeding, over eyes without protection, photosensitive skin conditions (SLE, porphyria), patients on photosensitising drugs (tetracyclines, NSAIDs, diuretics).

pearls.label: 🟡 Safety Pearl
pearls.text: Always protect eyes with appropriate eyewear during any colour lamp therapy. Start with 10 min sessions and observe patient response before extending to 30 min. Document colour used, duration, and patient response at each session. In photosensitive patients, start with cool colours (blue/green) at lower intensity.

cards._legacyHtml:
Absolute Contraindications
Never apply red/infrared light to: active cancer sites, acute inflammation/infection, areas of active bleeding, over eyes without protection, photosensitive skin conditions (SLE, porphyria), patients on photosensitising drugs (tetracyclines, NSAIDs, diuretics).
ColourAvoid InReason
Red/OrangeHypertension, fever, inflammation, anxiety, epilepsyFurther stimulation worsens condition
BlueDepression, hypothyroidism, cold/cyanosisFurther sedation contraproductive
YellowAcute fever, diarrhoea, nauseaStimulates already overactive GI system
VioletPsychosis, maniaMay exacerbate symptoms
🟡 Safety Pearl
Always protect eyes with appropriate eyewear during any colour lamp therapy. Start with 10 min sessions and observe patient response before extending to 30 min. Document colour used, duration, and patient response at each session. In photosensitive patients, start with cool colours (blue/green) at lower intensity.

cards.title: Chromotherapy — Disease Protocols & LED Evidence

rows: Blue lamp + block blue at night
Strong RCT — kills P.acnes + reduces inflammation

pearls.label: 🟡 Photobiomodulation (PBM) — The Evidence Base
pearls.text: PBM (red and near-infrared light 630–1000nm) is the most evidence-based form of chromotherapy. Mechanism: cytochrome c oxidase (complex IV in mitochondrial electron transport chain) absorbs red/NIR photons → increased ATP production, reactive oxygen species signalling, nitric oxide release. Clinical applications with strong RCT evidence: wound healing, musculoskeletal pain, post-surgical tissue repair, hair regrowth (LLLT), neck pain. Dose matters: too little = no effect, too much = inhibitory (biphasic dose response).

cards._legacyHtml:
ConditionColourMethodDurationEvidence
Seasonal Affective DisorderFull-spectrum white / 10,000 luxSAD lamp, 30cm distance30 min each morningCochrane: equivalent to antidepressants
DepressionYellow + Orange lampMorning lamp session30 min dailyGood — stimulates serotonin
InsomniaBlue lamp + block blue at nightEvening blue lamp + blackout20 min at 8pmGood — melatonin pathway
Acne vulgarisBlue 415nm + Red 630nm LEDLED device over face15 min BDStrong RCT — kills P.acnes + reduces inflammation
Wound healingRed 630–660nm + NIR 850nmLED over wound10 min ODStrong — photobiomodulation
Chronic arthritis painRed / InfraredIR lamp local20–30 min ODGood RCT evidence
Acute inflammationBlue lamp localBlue lamp local15–20 min BDModerate — anti-inflammatory
HypertensionBlue + GreenLamp + solarised water25 min + 100ml TDSModerate — ANS modulation
🟡 Photobiomodulation (PBM) — The Evidence Base
PBM (red and near-infrared light 630–1000nm) is the most evidence-based form of chromotherapy. Mechanism: cytochrome c oxidase (complex IV in mitochondrial electron transport chain) absorbs red/NIR photons → increased ATP production, reactive oxygen species signalling, nitric oxide release. Clinical applications with strong RCT evidence: wound healing, musculoskeletal pain, post-surgical tissue repair, hair regrowth (LLLT), neck pain. Dose matters: too little = no effect, too much = inhibitory (biphasic dose response).
